content:
The main symbol in the short story is the “worn path.” The “worn path” is a representation of the actual path Phoenix took into town as well as her emotional and spiritual journey. The “worn path” developed its name from the continuous journeys that Phoenix took on it despite her age also shining light on her constant determination to make it to town. This determination to arrive at her destination to retrieve the medicine her grandson desperately needed symbolizes sacrifice and the distance she was willing to go for someone she loved. The consistent pattern of the same journey emphasizes selfless commitment, regardless of the physical and emotional challenges that arise.

The thorn bush is the second crucial symbol. Phoenix ends up twisted in it and says, “It was the bush, and the bush was all over her.” The representation of the bush is the struggles that Phoenix faces in life, physically and societally. Regardless of the pain she encounters, she continues to push forward and take care of the things she needs to. The thorn bush holds a lot of representation despite the struggles, it shines light on her strength and will power to continue to complete her goal.
